topics.title: Dirty Cop Stories and Tales of Corruption
topics.summary: The studio explores the experiences of a former officer who engaged in illegal activities, including running a gambling operation and accepting payoffs from drug dealers.

topics.title: Police Stories and Cultural Slang
topics.summary: The studio features stories about police interactions, including an officer's prank with a fake kangaroo and a humorous account of transporting prisoners while playing Led Zeppelin.

topics.title: Legal Dispute with Les Moonves
topics.summary: The studio discusses the legal battle against Les Moonves, characterizing his actions as a personal vendetta and criticizing his management of CBS.

topics.title: CBS Lawsuit Defense Strategy
topics.summary: The studio discusses the legal strategy against a lawsuit from CBS, highlighting potential counterclaims for frivolous litigation and invasion of privacy.

topics.title: CBS Lawsuit Against Howard Stern
topics.summary: The studio discusses the legal battle between Howard Stern and CBS, characterizing the lawsuit as a personal vendetta and a strategic distraction from declining radio ratings.

topics.title: Legal Dispute with CBS and Les Moonves
topics.summary: The studio discusses the ongoing legal battle against CBS and executive Les Moonves, highlighting a specific interview tape as evidence of professional betrayal and personal vindication.

topics.title: Rivalry and Sirius Success
topics.summary: The studio explores Howard Stern's history with radio imitators, his successful transition to satellite radio despite analyst skepticism, and the potential for airing show replays throughout the day.

topics.title: Identity and Sexual Fantasy
topics.summary: The show features a conversation with author Nora Vincent regarding her experiences as a lesbian, the nuances of gender identity, and personal sexual fantasies.

topics.title: Gender Identity and Male Experience
topics.summary: The show features a woman who lived as a man for an extended period to write a book, discussing the differences in social dynamics, dating experiences, and physical perceptions between men and women.

topics.title: Gender Dynamics and Celebrity Commentary
topics.summary: The studio explores the differences between male and female friendships, discusses celebrity appearances in film, and reviews various radio segments including a prank call to Al Pacino.

topics.title: XM and Sirius Satellite Radio Competition
topics.summary: The studio examines an interview with a former XM executive who details how the company's strategic shift following Howard Stern's move to Sirius led to internal panic and a loss of competitive advantage.

topics.title: Death of Caller Cliff Palates
topics.summary: The show features a phone call from the mother of listener Cliff Palates to share details regarding his recent passing due to illness.

topics.title: Tributes to Cliff Pallett
topics.summary: The show features a series of tributes and personal anecdotes regarding the passing of Cliff Pallett, including discussions on his life, his role in the Wackpack, and plans for a memorial film.

topics.title: Jermaine Jackson Book Controversy and Celebrity News
topics.summary: The studio discusses a stalled book project involving Jermaine Jackson, the recovery of journalist Bob Woodruff, actress Tricia Helfert's legal actions, and the passing of Dana Reeve.

topics.title: Dana Reeve's Death and Celebrity Reactions
topics.summary: The show covers the passing of Dana Reeve, featuring reactions from various celebrities regarding her battle with cancer and the future of her family.

topics.title: Media Industry Criticisms and Cultural News
topics.summary: The studio discusses the decline of satellite radio, corporate leadership at CBS, gender pay gaps, SAT scoring errors, and various upcoming comedy performances.
